pages.text:
In Lemonade Wars, you play as neighborhood kids
    competing to be the most successful lemonade
    magnate. In the process, you’ll employ questionable
    business tactics, throw tantrums, and involve the
    adults when necessary in order to win.
    Each player starts with some parental seed money,
    then they are off to the streets to peddle some sweet,
    sweet lemonade. Players receive victory points
    by earning titles and Bragging Rights via their
    accomplishments and sales during the game.



    Play progresses until 1 player ends their turn with
    3 victory points, either from First Dibs titles or
    Bragging Rights. The round is then completed
    so everyone has an equal number of turns. Lastly,
    final victory points are assigned. The player with the
    most victory points wins!




2

1 Rulebook            69 Lemon       69 Black         2 Status
                        Cards       Market Cards       Cards




21 Bargain            18 Classic    15 Gourmet      6 Turf Cards
 Stands                Stands         Stands




11 Bragging            16 First      20 Lemon       5 Player Aids
   Rights             Dibs Titles    Lord Titles




        70 Paper Money               1 Sale Die    2 Timeout Cards
   30 $1, 20 $2, 10 $5, 10 $10




                                                                     3

Market Setup




    1. Place the Bragging Rights cards in a face-up deck
       with their prices ordered from lowest on top to
       highest on the bottom
    2. Shuffle the First Dibs title cards and place a number
       of face-up cards equal to the number of players
       plus one next to the Bragging Rights. Return the
       unused cards to the box
    3. Shuffle the Lemon deck (including the Timeout
       cards) and place it face down below the Bragging
       Rights cards. Leave room to the left for discards
    4. Separate the 3 Stand types, shuffle each stack, and
       place them next to the Lemon deck
    5. Place the Whiniest Baby and Spoiled Rotten
       status cards next to the Stands
    6. Shuffle the Black Market deck and deal out 4 cards
       face up next to it (or 5 cards in a 2-player game).
       Leave room to the left for discards
    7. Place the money and sale die to the right of the market

    8. Shuffle the Lemon Lord title cards and place them

4     off to the side

Player Setup
9. Give each player:
   a. 3 Lemon Lord cards
      (keep 2, discard 1)
    ⚬   Place the rest in the box
  b. A player aid card
  c. A random Turf card
  d. $15 (the amount of money players have is public knowledge)
  e. 5 cards from the Lemon deck
    ⚬ If a player has a Timeout card in their hand, shuffle it
        back into the deck and draw a new card
10. Randomly determine the first player. Optionally,
  choose the player who most recently had lemonade
  a. Give the first player $1 extra for each other player
     in the game. Give the next player that amount
     minus 1 and continue until you reach the last
     player (who gets no extra money). As an example,
     in a 4 player game the 1st player gets +$3, the 2nd
     gets +$2, and the 3rd gets +$1
Start of the Game
Each player is able to buy 2 Stands and 2 Black Market
cards before the game commences.
Beginning with the first player, each player must
purchase one Stand and may optionally purchase one
Black Market card. Proceed clockwise to the next
player, refilling the Black Market as players make
purchases. Repeat until the last player has chosen their
cards. Then, repeat this process in counter-clockwise
order starting with the last player until each player has
gone twice. The game is now ready to begin.


                                                                  5

Each player’s turn is split into 3 phases:
    • Start: Draw 1 Lemon card
    • Play: Perform up to 2 actions (instants are not actions)
    • Sell: Roll sale die and make money / trigger effects

    Actions
    Each player may perform up to 2 actions during their
    Play phase. Actions may be repeated. Only actions
    denoted with an asterisk (*) may be tantrummed.
    • Draw 1 Lemon card
        ⚬ There is no hand limit, and the amount of cards players have
          is public knowledge
        ⚬ Shuffle the discard pile if the deck runs out

    •   Play 1 plan        or attack       Lemon card*
        ⚬   For a more frenzied, action-filled game: Play up to 2 cards
    •   Purchase & equip 1 Black Market card*
        ⚬   Refill the Black Market after purchasing a card
        ⚬   Shuffle the discard pile if the deck runs out
    • Purchase & play 1 Stand
    • Purchase Bragging Rights (limit 1 per turn)

    Free Actions
    Each player may perform free actions during their
    Play phase. These do not count towards their 2 main
    actions and may be tantrummed if denoted with an
    asterisk (*).
    • Play an instant   Lemon card*
        ⚬   This may be performed any number of times per turn
    •   Pay $1 to the bank to refresh the Black Market
        ⚬   This may only be performed once per turn
6

Each player has a Turf card that
represents their region of lemonade
sale control. Turf anatomy is
described below:
1. Turf Black Market cards are tucked above and
   behind your Turf card (max 5, as shown on the card)
2. Its power pour number and base power pour ability
   are shown at the top
3. Victory point cards are placed to its left
4. Stands are placed to its right
5. Played tantrum cards are placed below it

Power Pours
Each Turf card has a number on it. This is your power
pour number. Certain cards will trigger effects based
on a power pour, and others will add to the total
number of power pour numbers you have.
When a sale roll matches your power pour number,
trigger all Turf and Black Market power pour effects.
Your base Turf power pour ability lets you take $1 from
the bank.




                                                          7

Stand anatomy is described below:

    1. Stand   Black Market cards are
       tucked above and behind the Stand
       (up to the limit shown). The shape
       behind the icon is used for certain First Dibs cards
    2. One or more sale numbers that the Stand sells
       lemonade on when rolled
    3. Base money you gain when the Stand sells
    4. Base price to purchase
    5. Stand type

    New Stands
    Each Stand costs the base price plus $1 for each Stand
    that you already own. To acquire a new Stand, simply
    pay the total price to the bank and move the Stand
    into your Turf. You may insert the Stand wherever you
    want but may not move it once it is placed.

    For example, if you have
    2 Stands and you want
    to buy a Classic Stand, it
    will cost $5 ($3 base plus
    $2 for the 2 Stands
    you already own).
    There is no limit
    to the number
    of Stands you
    may own.

8

Black Market Cards
Stands can be equipped with cards from the Black
Market, up to the limit listed in the upper left corner.
When you attach a Black Market card, simply tuck it
behind the Stand so that the new effects are showing.

Earnings
When a sale number on a Stand is rolled during a sale
roll, you gain the amount of dollars listed. Then, trigger
any Black Market cards that are equipped to the
Stand.



The Black Market represents illicit
items and tactics used to upgrade
your Stands and your Turf. The shape
behind the icon in the top left is used
for certain First Dibs cards.
When you purchase a Black Market card, pay the
listed price to the bank then immediately play that
card on a Stand or Turf , depending on its type.
Black Market cards can only be applied
as long as the Turf
or Stand has fewer
cards than its limit.
You may choose to
discard an existing
Black Market card
to make room for your
purchase.

                                                             9

There are 3 types of Lemon cards: plans, attacks, and
     instants.
     Plans
     Plan cards have a blue border with a
     cog icon in the top-left corner and
     can be played with a standard action
     during your turn. To do so, lay the card
     on the table, carry out the text on the
     card, then discard it.
     Attacks
     Attack cards have a red border with
     a starburst icon in the top-left corner
     and can be played as a standard action
     on your turn. Choose another player to
     be the target of the attack, carry out
     the text on the card, then discard it.
     Instants
     Instant cards have a yellow border with
     a lightning icon in the top-left corner
     and can be played as a free action on
     your turn or in response to something
     happening. Some of them can even
     be played when it’s not your turn! The
     conditions to play an instant card are
     listed above the effects of the card.




10

Tantrums
Tantrums are a special type of instant card that allow
you to prevent another player from purchasing a Black
Market card or playing a Lemon card. You may not
tantrum Stand purchases or victory points, and you
may not tantrum abilities on existing Black Market
cards. After you play a tantrum, place it below your
Turf then discard the card the other player was playing
or buying. If it was a purchase, refund the money paid.
The player does not get their action back.

  NOTE: Players can go back and forth
  cancelling tantrums with tantrums. Each tantrum
  only cancels the card played immediately before it.
  All tantrums go to their owners’ tantrum pile, even
  if they were cancelled. The player whose tantrum
  wasn’t cancelled counts as gaining it last.

Whiniest Baby
After 1 or more tantrums are gained, give the player
with the most tantrums the Whiniest Baby card. You
may wait until all players are done playing tantrums in
response to each other. Note that this occurs after a
tantrum is gained, not necessarily played. If there is
a tie, the player who most recently gained a tantrum
takes the card. If that is unclear, have the tied players
roll the sale die. Whoever rolls lowest gains the card.
The Whiniest Baby card has negative effects when a
Timeout card is drawn but allows the player to draw 2
cards and discard 1 at the start of their turn instead of
only drawing 1 card.

                                                            11

Timeout
     When a Timeout card is drawn, immediately stop
     playing and follow the instructions on the card. This
     may not be tantrummed. All players must discard
     down to 10 Lemon cards in their hand. Additionally,
     the player with the Whiniest Baby card must pay $3
     for every tantrum card they have played (which should
     be below their Turf). If they cannot afford it, they must
     sell cards (at full base price) until they can. They do not
     get a refund if they sell more than is needed to pay.
     After that player has paid the bank, discard their
     played tantrum cards and the Timeout card. Pass
     the Whiniest Baby card to the player with the most
     tantrums (or back to the market if no players have
     tantrums). The player who originally drew the Timeout
     card may draw a new card, then play proceeds as usual.
     Selling Cards
     When an effect instructs you to sell cards, choose
     Black Market and/or Stand cards to sell then gain
     the amount of money specified by the effect. Return
     sold Black Market cards to the discard pile and sold
     Stands to the bottom of their respective stack. You
     may not sell cards unless an effect instructs you to.
     Spoiled Rotten
     Each time a victory point is gained, give the player with
     the fewest victory points the Spoiled Rotten card.
     Ties do not count; the player has to be in last place.
     This card allows them to make a free sale roll for only
     themselves at the start of each of their turns. Return
     the card to the market if there is a tie for last place.

12

At the end of a player’s turn, they roll the sale die and
everyone attempts to sell lemonade. You may not
tantrum the act of selling or Black Market abilities.
In order to sell:
1. The active player rolls the sale die. This roll applies
   to all players
2. Apply any modifiers on the roll from instant cards
   and/or Black Market cards
3. Each player gains the base earnings from each
   Stand that has a sale number matching the final
   result of the roll
4. Finally, trigger any power pour effects and Black
   Market cards from Turfs and Stands that sell
Play then proceeds clockwise to the next player.




There are three ways to earn victory points in the
game. Each title and Bragging Rights card is worth 1
victory point. Once earned, victory points may not be
stolen or taken away. Claiming victory points may not
be tantrummed.
Bragging Rights can be purchased for the cost
printed on the top card in the stack. The first card costs
$16, and each subsequent card costs an additional $2.
These cards are a great way to turn extra money into
victory points!

                                                             13

First Dibs titles allow you to claim a victory point as
     soon as you meet the printed goal. If multiple players
     achieve the goal at the same time, each of them gains
     the victory point. They may take an unused First Dibs
     card from the box to track their score if needed.
     Lemon Lord titles are secret victory points that
     are awarded if you end the game with the objective
     completed. There is no penalty for not completing an
     objective. This means you still have a chance to win if
     you didn’t trigger the end of the game!
     For titles that contain icons, you must have the exact
     icon to achieve the objective (except for Roll Wrangler).
     For example, you may not complete the Sales Engine
     Lemon Lord title (end with 3+ ) with the Lefty Loosey
     Black Market card because it has the        symbol.



     The end of the game is triggered when a player ends
     their turn with 3 victory points from First Dibs titles
     and/or Bragging Rights cards. Lemon Lord titles do
     not count towards triggering the end of the game.
     Finish the round so that all players have an equal
     number of turns, then reveal all Lemon Lord titles and
     add completed cards to your score. The player with
     the most victory points wins!
     If there is a tie, the player with the most money wins.
     If still tied, the player with the fewest played tantrums
     wins. If, by the lemon-gods, there is still a tie, play rock
     paper scissors (best 2 of 3) to determine the winner.
14

What happens if a player runs out of money?
If you are required to pay a penalty to the bank or
another player, you pay as much as you can but you
cannot go negative (except for Timeouts). You may
not buy a card if you don’t have enough money for it.
What’s the order of operations for resolving
abilities, Black Market cards, and attacks?
Players may resolve their own triggered effects in
any order. If a situation arises that requires a specific
order of operations, begin with the active player and
proceed clockwise.
Do I have to use the abilities on my Black Market
cards when they trigger?
You may choose to not use abilities if you don’t want
to, but you have to complete the full ability if you do.
Can I add an existing sale or power pour number
to a Stand or Turf with Pushy Salesman or Spiked
Lemonade to trigger it twice?
No. Adding a sale or power pour number the Stand or
Turf already has will have no incremental effect.
Scan below (or visit sophcerb.com/lmw_rules) to
view a living FAQ document!




                                                            15
